sources.name: Heerema Marine Contractors — Fleet & Equipment Folders
sources.format: HTML fleet pages + downloadable PDF equipment folders
sources.notes: Authoritative first-party. HMC Equipment Folder PDF (https://www.heerema.com/hubfs/HMC%20Equipment%20Folder_20211108.pdf) and per-vessel folder (e.g. Balder https://f.hubspotusercontent40.net/hubfs/8565747/HMC%20Equipment%20Folder%20-%20Balder.pdf) give principal dimensions, draft range, displacement, deck area/loading, crane tandem capacities. No RAOs or mass-property tables (radii of gyration) published.

sources.name: Saipem — Fleet & Yards vessel datasheets
sources.format: HTML fleet pages + PDF spec sheets
sources.notes: First-party PDFs: Saipem 7000 (https://www.saipem.com/sites/default/files/2023-01/Saipem_7000.pdf), Saipem FDS (https://www.saipem.com/sites/default/files/2023-01/Saipem_FDS.pdf). Cover dims, displacement, dual 7000t cranes/tandem 14000t, J-lay tensioner capacity, pipe diameter range, DP. No RAO/mass-property data.

sources.name: Allseas — Our Fleet vessel pages
sources.notes: Pioneering Spirit, Solitaire, Audacia, Lorelay, Fortitude. Gives LOA/beam/depth, S-lay tension capacity, topside/jacket lift capacity (Pioneering Spirit 48,000t/25,000t), pipe hold capacity. Per-vessel pages are HTML; downloadable detailed spec sheets are limited. No RAO/mass props.

sources.name: Boskalis — Heavy Lift Vessels equipment sheets
sources.notes: Bokalift 1 (https://boskalis.com/media/5rbfb1cp/bokalift_1_03.pdf) and Bokalift 2 (https://boskalis.com/media/cafdcmsf/bokalift-2.pdf) equipment sheets: dims, draft, crane capacity vs radius (e.g. 4000t @ 28m), lift height, deck area + deck loading (t/m2), accommodation, DP. Among the better public sources for deck-loading data.

sources.name: Subsea7 — Vessel datasheets
sources.notes: Per-vessel PDFs e.g. Seven Oceanic (construction/vertical-flex-lay) and Seven Merlin under /content/dam/subsea7-corporate2018/Datasheets/Vessel/. Cover dims, lay system (flex/reel/J), crane SWL, deck area, A&R, DP. Reel-lay scope well represented. No RAOs.

sources.name: McDermott — DLV 2000 / fleet (via Ship-Technology project page)
sources.freshness: Static (vessel delivered 2016; page not actively updated)
sources.notes: McDermott's own site has limited public spec PDFs, so Ship-Technology is the most complete public consolidation for DLV 2000: LOA 184m, beam 38.6m, draft 5.5-7.7m, 2200t revolving crane + 600t aux, S-lay 4.5-60in to 10,000ft, optional 500t flex-lay, DP3, 33MW, ~4000m2 deck, 400 POB. Secondary source; cross-check against McDermott press releases.

sources.name: Ship-Technology / Offshore-Energy / OEDigital / gCaptain (trade press)
sources.format: HTML editorial articles + project spec pages
sources.notes: Good gap-filler for vessels lacking detailed first-party PDFs and for SWZ Maritime engineering write-ups (e.g. Sleipnir crane internals https://swzmaritime.nl/news/2020/04/02/this-is-what-makes-the-cranes-of-the-sleipnir-special/). Editorial — verify numbers against operator sources.

sources.name: Cranepedia — Crane Vessel Load Charts
sources.notes: One of the few public sources with actual crane LOAD CHARTS (capacity vs radius) for vessels, not just headline SWL — e.g. Heerema Sleipnir, Pacific Osprey/Orca (WTIV). Source provenance per chart varies; treat as indicative and validate against operator data for engineering use.

sources.name: FreeCraneSpecs.com
sources.notes: Largest public crane load-chart DB. Primarily land/crawler/mobile cranes (the crane modules mounted on vessels, e.g. Huisman/Liebherr/NMF units) rather than vessels themselves — useful for the crane-unit capacity curves behind a vessel's deck crane. Less coverage of integrated lift vessels.

sources.name: Wikipedia — vessel & RAO articles
sources.notes: Quick consolidated principal particulars (displacement, dims, draft range) for SSCV Thialf/Sleipnir, Saipem 7000, Pioneering Spirit, plus the Response Amplitude Operator concept article (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Response_amplitude_operator). Good index/cross-check, not a primary engineering source; cite underlying refs.

sources.name: DNV — DNV-RP-C205 Environmental Conditions and Environmental Loads
sources.format: PDF standard (free download after DNV registration)
sources.notes: Authoritative methodology for wind/wave/current/sea-level statistical models, spectra (JONSWAP/Pierson-Moskowitz), short- and long-term metocean climate, and load/RAO calc guidance. DNV publishes its RPs/STs as free PDFs from dnv.com (registration). The de-facto basis for metocean design criteria and motion analysis inputs.

sources.name: DNV — DNV-ST-N001 Marine Operations and Marine Warranty
sources.notes: Governs operational metocean/weather-window criteria, alpha factors, operational vs design limits for transport, lift, and installation. Directly relevant to defining metocean acceptance criteria for installation-vessel operations. Free PDF via DNV.

sources.name: NOAA NDBC — National Data Buoy Center
sources.format: Real-time + historical data (web, text, NetCDF via THREDDS)
sources.notes: Free measured significant wave height, period, wind, direction at buoy stations. Primary observational source for site metocean and for validating hindcasts. US/Gulf of Mexico coverage strongest; global gaps. Point measurements (not gridded).

sources.name: ECMWF — ERA5 Reanalysis (Copernicus Climate Data Store)
sources.format: Gridded reanalysis (NetCDF/GRIB) via free CDS account + API
sources.notes: Predominant free global wind+wave hindcast for deriving 50/100-yr extreme metocean design criteria via extreme-value analysis. Free with registration; coarse resolution near coast — combine with NDBC for calibration. Hourly global coverage.

sources.name: BOEM — Metocean Characterization Recommended Practices for US Offshore Wind
sources.notes: Free government methodology for metocean data collection/characterization for offshore projects; useful framework for installation-support metocean criteria even though oriented to wind. Complements NREL technical reports (e.g. docs.nrel.gov).

sources.name: TU Delft Repository — RAO identification/calibration theses & papers
sources.notes: Closest public material to vessel motion RAOs: methodology for identifying/calibrating RAOs from full-scale measurements and directional wave spectra. Methodology and example data, NOT vessel-specific RAO libraries for the named fleet. Real RAOs for these vessels are proprietary to operators/warranty surveyors.

sources.name: VesselFinder / MarineTraffic (AIS)
sources.format: HTML AIS vessel pages (free tier) + paid API/history
sources.notes: Free IMO/flag/GT/LOA/beam and live position for identifying/confirming a specific hull (e.g. Saipem 7000 IMO 8501567). Particulars are coarse (no draft range, deck loading, crane curves). Good for hull ID and cross-referencing IMO numbers; deep history/API is paid.

paywalled_gaps: Clarksons Research — World Fleet Register / Offshore Intelligence Network (clarksons.net/oin): comprehensive offshore vessel fleet data, owners, specs, contracts — subscription only; the most complete commercial fleet register.
S&P Global / IHS Markit — Petrodata (formerly ODS-Petrodata, IHS RigPoint/Offshore Rig & vessel intelligence): paid offshore rig and vessel databases; no free spec access.
Enverus (formerly Drillinginfo) — offshore vessel/rig and project intelligence: paid subscription; not publicly browsable.

ABS / DNV / Lloyd's Register class registers (full vessel records, stability/loading data): class society detailed records are restricted to owners and authorized parties — only headline particulars are public.

Vendor crane load-capacity charts at engineering fidelity (Huisman, Liebherr Maritime, NOV/NMF) — full as-built capacity envelopes and dynamic-amplification curves are delivered to operators, not published; public versions are marketing summaries.

Vessel-specific motion RAO datasets and mass-property tables (displacement vs draft, KG, radii of gyration, hydrostatics) for the named fleet — held as proprietary class/warranty/operator data; not publicly available in any source found.

Site-specific commercial metocean hindcasts (Fugro/Oceanweather GOMOS/NEXTRA, BMT ARGOSS/Metocean-on-Demand, DHI) — calibrated regional design-criteria databases are paid; free ERA5/NDBC are the public substitutes.
recommended_priority: Heerema Marine Contractors — Fleet & Equipment Folders
Boskalis — Heavy Lift Vessels equipment sheets
DNV — DNV-RP-C205 Environmental Conditions and Environmental Loads
DNV — DNV-ST-N001 Marine Operations and Marine Warranty
ECMWF — ERA5 Reanalysis (Copernicus Climate Data Store)
NOAA NDBC — National Data Buoy Center
McDermott — DLV 2000 / fleet (via Ship-Technology project page)
BOEM — Metocean Characterization Recommended Practices for US Offshore Wind
TU Delft Repository — RAO identification/calibration theses & papers
